_meta.note: Common residential HVAC fault codes seeded from publicly available OEM service literature. Field techs must confirm against the actual service manual for the exact model before acting.
sources: Trane XL / XV communicating service manuals
Mitsubishi Electric Mr. Slim service manuals

13.meaning: Thermostat calling for heating and cooling simultaneously.
causes: Miswired thermostat (Y and W energized together)
Shorted thermostat cable between air handler and stat
13.fix: Meter at the control board — if Y and W are both hot with stat removed, short is in the cable. If only when stat is connected, replace stat.

14.meaning: Blower interlock / pressure switch failure during cooling.
causes: Blower not running (capacitor, ECM module, or bad relay)
Pressure switch tubing pinched or disconnected
Blocked combustion air intake (on 90+ furnaces sharing board)
14.fix: Confirm blower amp draw against nameplate. Inspect PS tubing and inducer pressure.

causes: Dirty filter / blocked return restricting airflow
Closed supply registers or collapsed flex duct
33.fix: Replace filter, verify airflow, test limit switch continuity at room temp (should be closed).

34.meaning: Ignition proving failure — burners failed to light within trial.
causes: Low gas pressure / closed upstream shutoff
34.fix: Check flame signal in µA (spec typ 1-10µA). Ohm HSI cold. Verify manifold pressure matches nameplate.

2_flash.meaning: External lockout — system has locked out after repeated retries.
causes: Ignition failure (see flame sensor / HSI / gas supply)
2_flash.fix: Clear call for heat and cycle power to reset. Diagnose root fault from the code that precedes lockout.

3_flash.meaning: Pressure switch stuck open with inducer running.
causes: Cracked inducer housing / weak inducer motor
3_flash.fix: Meter across PS during inducer run; inspect tubing and flue path.

4_flash.fix: Verify airflow and blower amps. Check manifold pressure.

E1.fix: Ohm thermistor at room temp; consult OEM resistance table. Replace if out of spec.

E2.fix: Reseat against coil return bend; replace if resistance out of spec.

E6.meaning: Communication failure between thermostat and air handler.
E6.fix: Verify A/B terminations; continuity end-to-end on each conductor.

causes: Harness unplugged at motor or control board
E200.fix: Inspect harness; swap ECM module if bench-tested bad.

E201.fix: Spin wheel by hand with power off — should turn freely. Replace motor if seized.

E223.fix: Measure PS closure during inducer run (typ -0.30 to -1.0 in.wc).

57.fix: Manifold test — compare suction pressure at ambient saturation.

93.fix: Clean coil, verify fan amps and rotation, check subcool.

44.fix: Clean flame sensor; ohm HSI; measure manifold pressure.

88.meaning: Flame sensed when none should be present.
causes: Flame sensor grounded causing false signal
88.fix: Safety issue — shut off gas at valve. Test gas valve with manometer; replace if leaking by.

E0.fix: Check controller-to-indoor wiring (non-polar 2-conductor); replace controller if needed.

causes: Broken conductor in line set whip
E5.fix: Continuity on all 3 comm conductors; meter DC across S2-S3 per OEM spec.

P4.fix: Clear drain line, test float with unit, replace if stuck.

A1.fix: Cycle power; if persistent, replace indoor main PCB.

A3.fix: Test drain pump operation; clear hose; replace float switch if stuck.

U4.meaning: Communication failure between indoor and outdoor units.
U4.fix: Verify F1/F2 continuity and polarity; check DIP-switch address.
